Officers caught up with Hansen minutes later at the Pascal Street Bridge over I-94.
She was found to be completely outside of the fence, although she was over a slanted and grassy embankment, which posed little risk of injury.
She was also still drinking from a bottle of tequila and grabbing at the handgun tucked in her waistband.
Later, it is discovered that the handgun was in fact a replica, fashioned from foil and duct tape.
SWAT arrived and lowered her down onto I-94.
Though she could have easily been shot by the trooper, she ultimately received probation.
As part of a plea deal, her charge of drunk driving was dismissed, along with her charge of fleeing police.
She was not convicted in this matter and instead received a stay of adjudication for making a threat of violence and is currently on probation for this case according to court records.
